skill.name: to-issues
skill.description: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices. Use when user wants to convert a plan into issues, create implementation tickets, or break down work into work.
skill.summary: Break plans into independently-grabbable issues using vertical tracer-bullet slices
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The backtick command at line 10 is a standard slash command invocation pattern. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are nonsensical misidentifications of YAML frontmatter, markdown syntax, and plain text. The skill is a documentation/planning tool that poses no security risks.

medium_findings.title: Slash Command Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill references running a slash command `/setup-matt-pocock-skills`. This is a standard skill invocation pattern, not dangerous shell execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick syntax in markdown documentation refers to slash commands, not Ruby execution. This is the standard way to document slash command invocations in Claude skills.

content.value_statement: Converting long plans or specs into issues can be time-consuming and inconsistent. This skill breaks any plan into independently-grabbable vertical slice issues that are ready for implementation.
actual_capabilities: Analyze plans, specs, or PRDs and identify vertical slice opportunities
Break work into AFK (can be implemented immediately) and HITL (requires human decisions) slices
Create properly structured issues with parent references, acceptance criteria, and blockers
Respect project-specific vocabulary and ADRs when naming issues
Publish issues in dependency order with appropriate triage labels
limitations: Requires the issue tracker setup to be configured first (via `/setup-matt-pocock-skills`)
Cannot access the issue tracker without proper permissions and authentication
Depends on existing context or issue references to understand the source material

use_cases.title: Product manager creates sprint backlog
use_cases.description: A product manager has a new PRD and needs it broken down into implementation-ready tickets for the engineering team.

use_cases.title: Tech lead decomposes architectural work
use_cases.description: A tech lead has an ADR approved and needs it converted into vertical slice issues that respect existing ADRs in the codebase.

use_cases.title: Senior engineer breaks down a spike
use_cases.description: After a research spike, a senior engineer converts findings into actionable implementation issues for the team.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a plan to issues
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a plan for [feature description]. Can you break it down into vertical slice issues using the to-issues skill?
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic usage when you have a plan in conversation

prompt_templates.title: Convert existing issue to sub-issues
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to break down issue #[number] into smaller implementation tickets. Use the to-issues skill to help structure this.
prompt_templates.scenario: When referencing an existing issue by number

prompt_templates.title: Convert spec file to issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is the spec file at [path]. Read it and use the to-issues skill to create a vertical slice breakdown.
prompt_templates.scenario: When providing a file path to the spec

prompt_templates.title: Iterate on issue breakdown
prompt_templates.prompt: Based on the current codebase at [path], review the proposed issue breakdown and suggest improvements to better align with existing patterns.

output_examples.input: Break down this PRD into issues: User authentication with OAuth2 and session management
output_examples.output:
Here is the proposed issue breakdown:

1. **AFK-001: OAuth2 integration setup**
   - Type: AFK
   - Blocked by: None
   - User stories: US-001

2. **AFK-002: Session storage and management**
   - Type: AFK
   - Blocked by: AFK-001
   - User stories: US-002, US-003

3. **HITL-003: Login UI design review**
   - Type: HITL
   - Blocked by: AFK-002
   - User stories: US-004

I have fetched issue #42. Based on the description and existing codebase patterns, here is the proposed breakdown:

1. **AFK-010: Database schema for user preferences**
   - Type: AFK
   - Blocked by: None

2. **HITL-011: API endpoint design**
   - Type: HITL
   - Blocked by: AFK-010

[Would you like to proceed with publishing these issues?]

best_practices: Ensure the issue tracker setup has been run before using this skill
Review the vertical slice breakdown with stakeholders before publishing
Start with smaller, more granular slices rather than large ones
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for purely horizontal slices that only touch one layer (e.g., database-only changes)
Do not skip the quiz step - user approval is important for ensuring the right granularity
Do not close or modify parent issues when publishing sub-issues

faq.question: What is a vertical slice vs. a horizontal slice?
faq.answer: A vertical slice cuts through all layers end-to-end (schema, API, UI, tests) to deliver a complete feature path. A horizontal slice would only touch one layer, like a database migration without UI or tests.

faq.question: What is the difference between HITL and AFK?
faq.answer: HITL (Human-In-The-Loop) issues require a human decision, like an architectural decision or design review. AFK issues can be implemented and merged without any human approval.

faq.question: Do I need to run setup before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you should run `/setup-matt-pocock-skills` first to configure the issue tracker and triage label vocabulary for your project.

faq.question: Can I break down an existing issue into sub-issues?
faq.answer: Yes, pass the issue number, URL, or path as an argument and the skill will fetch and analyze the existing issue before creating sub-issues.

faq.question: How are dependencies between issues handled?
faq.answer: The skill publishes issues in dependency order so you can reference real issue identifiers in the Blocked by field. This ensures dependencies are properly linked.

faq.question: What triage labels are applied to new issues?
faq.answer: The skill applies the needs-triage label so each issue enters the normal triage flow. This ensures proper prioritization and assignment happens through your existing process.
